chemistry.mechanism_type: photochemical acid generation followed by acid-base reaction
chemistry.scope_summary: Universal method applicable to diverse metal chalcogenides including Pt, Sb, Sn, Cu, and Mo-based chalcogenidometallates for DLP 3D printing of functional semiconductor materials

figures.caption: Fig. 2 | DLP printing of ChaM-based inks. a  Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) image of the printed line patterns of PtS 2 -based chalcogenidometallte (ChaM) ink with widths ranging from 100  μm to 25  μm.  b  SEM image of the printed circles, squares, and triangles of PtS 2 -based ChaM ink at different scales of tens to hun- dreds of micrometres.  c  SEM image of hundreds of printed square arr | Fig. 1 | Generalised optical printing of metal chalcogenide. a  Schematic of the digital light processing (DLP)-based optical printing of chalcogenidometallate (ChaM)-based inks. The DLP process that operates by reflecting light off digital micro-mirror devices selectively solidifies the photocurable metal chalcogenide inks and create complex 2D films and 2.5Darchitectures by 2D layer-by-layer

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: UV exposure at 365 or 405nm triggers photoacid generation
Selective patterning occurs through UV exposure
Loss of charge and precipitation follows proton release
Zeta potential changes from negative to positive after UV exposure
Particle size increases after UV treatment
Formation of precipitates upon UV exposure

translation_logic.batch_limitation: This is not a batch-to-flow translation study
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Not applicable - this study focuses on photochemical 3D printing technology

figure_descriptions: DLP printing scheme showing photochemical patterning process
Mechanistic scheme of photoacid generation and ChaM precipitation

notes: This paper describes a photochemical 3D printing process rather than traditional batch or flow chemistry. The process uses DLP technology to selectively cure chalcogenidometallate inks via photoacid generation. Most quantitative reaction parameters are not applicable to this materials science application. Light source information was derived from figure descriptions showing DLP setup with UV exposure at 365nm.
